macro.title: Social Security funding gap widens in 2026 trustees report
macro.what: The 2026 Trustees Report estimates Social Security's 75-year funding gap at 4.42% of taxable payroll, up from 3.82% a year earlier.

housing.title: Florida Dominates May’s Most Expensive Home Sales, Led by a $75 Million Boca Raton Deal
housing.what: A Boca Raton mansion within a private golf course community and two apartments in New York City are among May’s most expensive home sales
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: When will home sales finally return to normal?
housing.what: Home sales are near 4.2 million, with lock-in preventing about 870,000 sales in 2026 and only about 5.8% decaying annually.
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: Demand stop-loss: Can a court ruling revive H-1B buyer mojo?
housing.what: The court ruling on the $100,000 H-1B fee raises questions about localized housing demand, especially in markets such as Celina north of Dallas
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: New zoning laws won’t help housing starts grow
housing.what: May housing starts fell 15.4% to 1.177M, while completed new homes for sale hit 122,000, a level that often limits new building.
housing.why: Supply pipeline sets future inventory and construction activity.
housing.market: Stronger pipeline aids suppliers; oversupply later can weigh on pricing.

housing.title: SpaceX Employees Could Buy 2 in 5 San Antonio Homes With Their IPO Windfall
housing.what: The massive IPO would create enough wealth for SpaceX employees to buy roughly 40% of all the homes in San Antonio, one of the closest major metros to where the company is headquartered
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.
